task_completion.description: Open the FZK-Haus IFC model in BlenderBIM/Bonsai
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender interface showing the FZK-Haus 3D building model loaded in the viewport and Outliner.

task_completion.description: Model at least two distinct solid geometric volumes beneath the building footprint
task_completion.visual_evidence: 3D viewport showing at least two new solid objects (e.g., meshes, cubes, or slabs) positioned underneath the main building model (Z-elevation < 1.0m).
Object Properties panel (IFC Class section) or the Outliner showing the selected subsurface volumes classified specifically as 'IfcGeographicElement'.

task_completion.description: Assign at least two IFC materials with geotechnical names to the volumes
task_completion.visual_evidence: Material Properties tab or IFC Material panel showing assigned materials with names containing terms like 'Clay', 'Sand', 'Gravel', 'Rock', 'Bedrock', 'Soil', 'Silt', 'Limestone', or 'Earth'.

task_completion.description: Add a property set with specific geotechnical engineering properties and numerical values
task_completion.visual_evidence: Object Properties > IFC Object Properties > Property Sets panel showing a custom property set containing at least one property named 'BearingCapacity', 'FrictionAngle', 'SoilDensity', or 'Permeability' with an assigned numerical value.

task_completion.description: Save the enriched IFC project to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: File save dialog, Blender info notification, or terminal window showing the project being saved to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_geotech.ifc'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the BlenderBIM/Bonsai GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active use of Blender's 3D viewport, menus, and property panels rather than just executing a pre-written Python script in the terminal to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely created 3D geometry for the stratigraphy
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the process of adding meshes (e.g., Add > Mesh > Cube) and scaling/translating them in the 3D viewport, rather than just editing metadata of existing building elements.

integrity.description: Agent did not use a text editor to manually inject IFC data
integrity.visual_evidence: The IFC classifications, materials, and properties were added using BlenderBIM's interface panels, not by opening the .ifc file in a text editor (like nano or gedit) and typing the STEP physical file syntax.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The base model is AC20-FZK-Haus.ifc. The output file must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_geotech.ifc'. The task requires at least two solid volumes classified as 'IfcGeographicElement'. Materials must include geotechnical terms like 'Clay', 'Sand', 'Gravel', 'Rock', 'Bedrock', 'Soil', 'Silt', 'Limestone', or 'Earth'. The property set must include at least one of: 'BearingCapacity', 'FrictionAngle', 'SoilDensity', or 'Permeability' with a numerical value.
